1	//! Code generator for Lua 5.4.
2	//!
3	//! Ports `src/lcode.c` (1875 lines, ~60 functions) and the public interface
4	//! from `src/lcode.h`.
5	//!
6	//! Phase A: faithful logic translation — does not yet compile.
7	//!
8	//! TODO(port): FuncState.f is GcRef<LuaProto> per types.tsv but the code
9	//! generator mutates it directly.  Phase B must wrap LuaProto in
10	//! RefCell<LuaProto> (or similar) inside GcRef so that `fs.f.code`, `fs.f.k`,
11	//! etc. are mutable.  All `fs.f.<field>` accesses below assume direct mutability.
12	//!
13	//! TODO(port): LexState.L (the &mut LuaState back-pointer) was removed per
14	//! types.tsv.  Functions that call luaM_growvector / luaH_get / luaC_barrier /
15	//! luaO_rawarith carry an explicit `state: &mut LuaState` parameter here.
16	//! The caller (lua-parse) must thread it through.
17	
18	// C: /* $Id: lcode.c $ */
19	// C: #define lcode_c
20	// C: #define LUA_CORE
21	
22	use crate::opcodes::{
23	    Instruction, OpCode, OpMode,
24	    MAXARG_A, MAXARG_B, MAXARG_C, MAXARG_BX, MAXARG_AX, MAXARG_S_J,
25	    OFFSET_S_BX, OFFSET_S_J, OFFSET_S_C,
26	    NO_REG, MAXINDEXRK, LFIELDS_PER_FLUSH,
27	};
28	
29	use lua_types::error::LuaError;
30	use lua_types::value::LuaValue;
31	use lua_types::string::LuaString;
32	use lua_types::gc::GcRef;
33	use lua_types::tagmethod::TagMethod;
34	
35	// ─── PHASE B PLACEHOLDERS ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
36	//
37	// The following types live in `lua-parse` (`FuncState`, `ExprDesc`, `ExprKind`,
38	// `LexState`) and `lua-vm` (`LuaState`, `LuaTable`).  Importing them from those
39	// crates would create a dependency cycle (`lua-vm` depends on `lua-code`), so
40	// we keep stub shapes here in Phase B.  The real definitions land when those
41	// crates fully exist; the field/method signatures below are inferred from the
42	// concrete usage in `codegen.rs`.
43	
44	/// PHASE B: real definition lives in lua-types (`lua_types::proto::LuaProto`),
45	/// but it stores `Vec<lua_types::opcode::Instruction>` — a barebones u32 wrapper
46	/// without the `arg_a` / `set_opcode` / `is_in_top` / etc. methods that the
47	/// code generator calls.  Those methods live on the local
48	/// `crate::opcodes::Instruction` type.  Until the two `Instruction` types are
49	/// unified (Phase B), `FuncState` carries a local proto shape that uses the
50	/// methodful local `Instruction`.
51	pub struct LuaProto {
52	    pub numparams: u8,
53	    pub is_vararg: bool,
54	    pub maxstacksize: u8,
55	    pub k: Vec<LuaValue>,
56	    pub code: Vec<Instruction>,
57	    pub lineinfo: Vec<i8>,
58	    pub abslineinfo: Vec<AbsLineInfo>,
59	}
60	
61	/// PHASE B: mirrors `lua_types::proto::AbsLineInfo`; kept local so it pairs
62	/// with the local `LuaProto` placeholder above.
63	#[derive(Clone, Copy)]
64	pub struct AbsLineInfo {
65	    pub pc: i32,
66	    pub line: i32,
67	}
68	
69	/// PHASE B: real definition lives in lua-parse; placeholder shape inferred
70	/// from codegen usage.
71	pub struct FuncState {
72	    pub f: LuaProto,
73	    pub pc: i32,
74	    pub lasttarget: i32,
75	    pub previousline: i32,
76	    pub iwthabs: u8,
77	    pub nabslineinfo: i32,
78	    pub nactvar: u8,
79	    pub freereg: u8,
80	    pub nk: i32,

delta.text: Test passes all
4 steps and "execution completed, status=Ok". The original panic at `crates/lua-parse/src/lib.rs:3019

:5` is gone.

## Report

**Root cause:** `adjust_assign` in `crates/lua-parse/src/lib.rs:1340
` had its register/codegen calls stubbed as `// TODO(port)
` comments. For `local x = 5;`, the C code calls `luaK_exp2next
reg(fs, e)` which reserves a register, increments `freereg` to 1, and emits a `LOADI` instruction. The st
ubbed Rust version did none of that, leaving `freereg=0` while `nvarstack=1` after `adjust_local_vars` bumped `nactvar`

to 1. The debug assertion at line 3019 then caught the mismatch (`freereg >= nvarstack` failed: `0 >= 1`).

**Fix:**
Wired `adjust_assign` to call the existing local helper `cg_exp_to_next_reg(fs, line
, e)` when `e.k != Void` and non-mult-ret. Also implemented `cg_emit_nil
` for the `needed > 0` (missing rhs values) path, and replaced the no-op `re
serve_regs` TODO with `needed` calls to `reserve_reg`. The mult-ret branch is still a TODO since
it requires `set_returns` (not needed for this test). Removed all debug `eprintln!` inst

rumentation.

**New status:** Test now completes all 4 steps including execution (`[ok] execution completed, status=Ok`)
— no remaining error. Debug trace confirmed: after `local x = 5`,
`freereg=1`, `nvarstack=1`; after `print(x)`, `freereg=2`, `nvarstack=1`, and the post
-statement reset clamps `freereg` back to `nvarstack=1`.

## Report

**Root cause:** `adjust_assign` in `crates/lua-parse/src/lib.rs:1340` had its register/codegen calls stubbed as `// TODO(port)` comments. For `local x = 5;`, the C code calls `luaK_exp2nextreg(fs, e)` which reserves a register, increments `freereg` to 1, and emits a `LOADI` instruction. The stubbed Rust version did none of that, leaving `freereg=0` while `nvarstack=1` after `adjust_local_vars` bumped `nactvar` to 1. The debug assertion at line 3019 then caught the mismatch (`freereg >= nvarstack` failed: `0 >= 1`).

**Fix:** Wired `adjust_assign` to call the existing local helper `cg_exp_to_next_reg(fs, line, e)` when `e.k != Void` and non-mult-ret. Also implemented `cg_emit_nil` for the `needed > 0` (missing rhs values) path, and replaced the no-op `reserve_regs` TODO with `needed` calls to `reserve_reg`. The mult-ret branch is still a TODO since it requires `set_returns` (not needed for this test). Removed all debug `eprintln!` instrumentation.

**New status:** Test now completes all 4 steps including execution (`[ok] execution completed, status=Ok`) — no remaining error. Debug trace confirmed: after `local x = 5`, `freereg=1`, `nvarstack=1`; after `print(x)`, `freereg=2`, `nvarstack=1`, and the post-statement reset clamps `freereg` back to `nvarstack=1`.

result:
Test passes all 4 steps and "execution completed, status=Ok". The original panic at `crates/lua-parse/src/lib.rs:3019:5` is gone.

## Report

**Root cause:** `adjust_assign` in `crates/lua-parse/src/lib.rs:1340` had its register/codegen calls stubbed as `// TODO(port)` comments. For `local x = 5;`, the C code calls `luaK_exp2nextreg(fs, e)` which reserves a register, increments `freereg` to 1, and emits a `LOADI` instruction. The stubbed Rust version did none of that, leaving `freereg=0` while `nvarstack=1` after `adjust_local_vars` bumped `nactvar` to 1. The debug assertion at line 3019 then caught the mismatch (`freereg >= nvarstack` failed: `0 >= 1`).

**Fix:** Wired `adjust_assign` to call the existing local helper `cg_exp_to_next_reg(fs, line, e)` when `e.k != Void` and non-mult-ret. Also implemented `cg_emit_nil` for the `needed > 0` (missing rhs values) path, and replaced the no-op `reserve_regs` TODO with `needed` calls to `reserve_reg`. The mult-ret branch is still a TODO since it requires `set_returns` (not needed for this test). Removed all debug `eprintln!` instrumentation.

**New status:** Test now completes all 4 steps including execution (`[ok] execution completed, status=Ok`) — no remaining error. Debug trace confirmed: after `local x = 5`, `freereg=1`, `nvarstack=1`; after `print(x)`, `freereg=2`, `nvarstack=1`, and the post-statement reset clamps `freereg` back to `nvarstack=1`.
